market_session: US regular session close on 2026-06-23

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from the AP closing-index ledger; daily moves are versus the prior regular session of 2026-06-22

text: US stocks fell broadly on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, led by technology. The S&P 500 fell 107.33 points (1.4%) to 7,365.46; the Nasdaq Composite fell 579.56 points (2.2%) to 25,587.04; the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which carries less technology weighting, fell 45.87 points (0.1%) to 51,666.84; and the Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points (about 1%) to 2,975.48.

subject.event: fell back below 3,000 one session after first-ever close above it

market_session: US regular session close on 2026-06-23

calculation.method: today's AP close minus the 2026-06-22 close (3,004.40) equals -28.92 points; the index closed back below the 3,000 level it had cleared for the first time the prior session

text: The small-cap Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points (about 1%) to 2,975.48, slipping back below 3,000 just one session after its first-ever close above that level (3,004.40) on June 22. The milestone was given back as the broad risk-off tech selloff spread.

value.basis: Micron is the AP reported close; the other chip percentages are intraday snapshots from TheStreet's live blog and may differ from final closes

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23 (Micron close; other chip moves intraday)
text: Semiconductors led the decline. Micron Technology, which reports earnings the next day, lost 13.2% per the AP ledger. Intraday on TheStreet's live blog, Qualcomm fell about 8.6%, Arm Holdings about 8.4%, Taiwan Semiconductor about 5.2%, SanDisk about 11.7%, and Nvidia about 2.7%; those intraday figures are carried at medium confidence and may differ from final closes.

value.kospi_note: steepest drop in more than three months
value.trading_halt: automatic 20-minute halt triggered on the Korea exchange
value.catalyst: regulatory signals that the chip rally had become overheated (Reuters, cited by TheStreet)

market_session: Asian session on 2026-06-23 ahead of the US open
text: The selloff originated overseas. South Korea's Kospi plunged 9.99%, its steepest drop in more than three months, after regulators cautioned that the chip rally had become overheated; Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each tumbled more than 12%, triggering an automatic 20-minute trading halt. That rout fed directly into US semiconductor and AI-linked names.

value.theme: AI trade cooling / questions over overstretched AI valuations
value.proximate_trigger: Broadcom results reportedly failed to deliver the guidance boost markets expected, per Hargreaves Lansdown's Anna Macdonald, triggering a US semiconductor selloff that fed into Asia
value.record_context: the AI-led rally had carried indexes to record highs into early June

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23
text: The proximate driver was AI-trade fatigue. Yahoo Finance framed the day as the AI trade cooling off with semiconductors retreating, and TheStreet cited Hargreaves Lansdown strategist Anna Macdonald attributing the move to Broadcom's results failing to deliver the guidance boost markets expected, which sparked a US chip selloff that fed into Asia overnight. The AP noted the selling was concentrated in AI-related companies that had run up to record after record, most recently in early June.

value.bofa_note: Bank of America research note flagged no rate cuts until 2028 and as many as three rate hikes this year
value.ap_framing: investors worried the growing likelihood of rate hikes this year to fight inflation will weigh on economic growth
value.us2y_note: 2-year yield hit its highest since February 2025
value.oil: oil prices fell even as yields stayed elevated

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23
text: A macro overhang amplified the selling. TheStreet highlighted a Bank of America note portending no rate cuts until 2028 and up to three rate hikes this year, and the AP framed the day around worries that a growing likelihood of rate hikes to fight inflation will weigh on growth. The 10-year Treasury yield sat around 4.495% and the 2-year reached its highest since February 2025, even as oil prices fell. Yield and BofA figures are intraday and carried at medium confidence.

subject.topic: narrow tech-led decline with broader internals firmer

value.basis: single-stock figures are intraday or premarket snapshots from TheStreet's live blog

market_session: US regular session on 2026-06-23 (single-stock figures intraday/premarket)
text: The decline was narrow: TheStreet reported the S&P 500's technology sector down about 4.1% even as roughly 299 of the index's 500 holdings traded higher, and the Dow's lighter tech weighting limited it to a 0.1% loss. Defensive and software names plus some mega-cap tech bucked the slide, with Microsoft up about 2.5% and Amazon up about 1.7% intraday, and IBM (+4.2%), Accenture (+3.3%) and Public Storage (+4.4%) higher premarket. These single-stock figures are intraday or premarket snapshots and are carried at medium confidence.

value.cross_market_context: normal regular session for both European and US venues; European equities followed a global tech-led risk-off move after weakness on Wall Street and rising US Federal Reserve rate-hike bets

market_session: European regular cash-equity session, Tuesday 2026-06-23 (closed)
text: European equity markets traded a normal regular session on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, and closed lower in a broad, tech-led risk-off move. Live closing prints are evidenced by the LSEG FTSE 100 daily table (close as of 16:35 BST on volume of about 886 million shares) and dated June 23 closes across the Trading Economics Euro Area, Germany and France pages. Daily moves are measured against the Monday, June 22 close. Unlike the prior report date, there is no US-holiday asymmetry: both European and US venues traded a normal session, and European equities followed weakness on Wall Street and rising US Federal Reserve rate-hike expectations.

market_session: European regular close on 2026-06-23; FTSE 100 at the 16:35 BST London close

calculation.method: FTSE 100: June 23 close 10,428.85 minus June 22 close 10,437.85 equals -9.00 points (-0.09%); continental percentages are Trading Economics CFD-tracked day-changes vs the prior session, corroborated by the Reuters wrap except for the DAX, where Reuters cites -0.3% at the 17:30 CET Xetra cash close versus the Trading Economics CFD -0.98%

text: European equities closed broadly lower on Tuesday, June 23, 2026 in a tech-led selloff. The pan-European STOXX Europe 600 ended 634.64, down 0.72% (Reuters -0.7%; MarketScreener 634.63 / -0.73%). The EURO STOXX 50 fell 1.25% to 6,232.46 from 6,311.32. France's CAC 40 dropped 0.71% to 8,340.71, a third straight decline. The UK's FTSE 100 was the relative outperformer, off just 9.00 points (-0.09%) to a precise 10,428.85. Germany's DAX carries a disclosed vendor discrepancy: the Trading Economics CFD-tracked DE40 closed 24,893.58, down 246.11 points (-0.98%) from 25,139.69, while the Reuters European cash-close wrap put the DAX at -0.3%; the gap reflects basis (Reuters 17:30 CET Xetra cash close versus the Trading Economics CFD extending into the post-cash session as the US tech selloff deepened). Daily moves are measured against the June 22 close.

market_session: London regular session close, Tuesday 2026-06-23 (16:35 BST)

calculation.method: close 10,428.85 minus prior close 10,437.85 equals -9.00 points; -9.00 / 10,437.85 equals -0.09 percent

text: The FTSE 100 is the cycle's one precise official close and the day's relative outperformer: on the Investors Chronicle/LSEG historical daily table it opened 10,438.24, traded a 10,332.40 to 10,462.19 range, and closed 10,428.85 on volume of 886,362,424 shares, down just 9.00 points (-0.09%) from Monday's 10,437.85 - well ahead of the continental indexes, whose deeper losses tracked the technology selloff (Reuters put the FTSE 100 at -0.2%).

subject.name: Global tech selloff and rising Fed rate-hike bets drive European equities lower

value.primary_support: global technology-sector selloff on AI-capex and valuation concerns, plus rising expectations of imminent US Federal Reserve rate hikes
triggers: SpaceX raising more cash after its IPO
SK Hynix signalling slower AI-chip output to favour commodity DRAM
value.rates: markets priced roughly 50 bps of Fed hikes by year-end and an additional 25 bps from the ECB
value.oil_iran: US Treasury issued a 60-day license authorizing sale of Iranian oil, pushing crude to a three-month low
value.macro: euro-area June flash PMI showed private-sector activity contracting for a third straight month; German activity shrank the most since 2024

market_session: European regular session, Tuesday 2026-06-23
text: European shares fell on Tuesday in a broad risk-off move led by a global technology selloff and rising bets on imminent US Federal Reserve rate hikes. Per Reuters, expectations for Fed hikes and concerns over corporate AI spending dented sentiment, with the STOXX 600 tech sector down 3.7%, its biggest daily drop since February. Trading Economics tied the chip-led leg to SpaceX raising more cash after its IPO and SK Hynix signalling it would slow AI-chip production to favour commodity DRAM, with markets pricing about 50 bps of Fed hikes by year-end and a further 25 bps from the ECB. A US Treasury 60-day license authorizing sale of Iranian oil pushed crude to a three-month low, and euro-area June flash PMI showed private-sector activity contracting for a third straight month - the steepest contraction in Germany since 2024.

laggards.technology_note: biggest weight on the STOXX 600; biggest daily drop since February
laggards.industrials_datacenter_exposed: Schneider Electric -4.53%, Siemens Energy -3.93%, Hochtief -4.3%, Siemens -2.42%

leaders.pharma_note: Sanofi up about 1.5% after EU approval of its Cenrifki treatment

market_session: European regular session, Tuesday 2026-06-23
text: The session was a clean defensives-over-cyclicals rotation. Technology was the biggest weight on the STOXX 600, down 3.7% in its biggest daily drop since February, and mining fell 3.3% with lower precious-metal prices, per Reuters. Industrials with datacenter exposure were hit hard on Trading Economics data - Schneider Electric -4.53%, Siemens Energy -3.93%, Hochtief -4.3% and Siemens -2.42%. Defensives outperformed: healthcare rose 1.9% and food and beverage 1.7%, with Sanofi up about 1.5% after the EU approved its Cenrifki treatment. Sector and single-stock percentages are carried at medium confidence; the index-level closes are high confidence.

market_session: European regular session, Tuesday 2026-06-23
text: Chipmakers led the decliners: STMicroelectronics fell about 8.1% to 8.5% (Trading Economics France -8.1%; Reuters -8.5%), Infineon -6.26% to 80.56, and ASML -5.74%. Datacenter- and capex-exposed industrials followed - Schneider Electric -4.53%, Hochtief -4.3%, Siemens Energy -3.93% and Siemens -2.42% - while Vonovia fell 3.21% after announcing a convertible-bond issue. Gainers were led by Airbus (+1.97%, intraday wrap +2.2%), SAP (+1.74% on the table, +2.13% in the Trading Economics news item), Qiagen (+1.51%) and Sanofi (+1.35%); luxury was mixed, with LVMH up 0.49%. Single-stock percentages are carried at medium confidence.

value.halt_trigger: KOSPI down more than 8% from previous session close
value.record_context: fourth time this year and 10th time on record that KRX halted all stocks for 20 minutes

market_session: South Korea regular equity session close on 2026-06-23
text: South Korea's KOSPI plunged 910.71 points, or 9.99%, to close at 8,203.84 on June 23 after hitting an intraday high of 9,175.45; the Korea Exchange triggered a market-wide circuit breaker around 2:33 p.m. local time after the index dropped more than 8%, halting all stocks for 20 minutes.

market_session: South Korea regular equity session close on 2026-06-23; won quote as of 15:30 KST
text: Korea's selloff was chip-led: Samsung Electronics fell 12.31% and SK Hynix fell 12.47%, with UPI/Yonhap reporting foreign and institutional net selling of 4.13 trillion won and 4.55 trillion won against 8.58 trillion won of retail net buying. Anadolu also reported decliners outnumbering gainers 856 to 46, turnover of 59.9 trillion won, Samsung Electro-Mechanics down 9.6%, Hyundai Motor down 12.05%, LG Energy Solution down 6.1%, and the won at 1,539.1 per dollar as of 3:30 p.m.

market_session: Japan regular equity session close on 2026-06-23

calculation.method: implied percent move = reported point change divided by prior close inferred as reported close minus reported point change; Nikkei: -2565.58/(69788.38+2565.58); TOPIX: -104.67/(3990.38+104.67)

text: Japan sold off at the June 23 Tokyo close: Jiji Press reported the Nikkei Average at 69,788.38, down 2,565.58 points, and TOPIX at 3,990.38, down 104.67 points. Those point moves imply declines of about 3.55% and 2.56%, respectively, versus the prior close.

market_session: Asia session and later global session on 2026-06-23
text: The Asia move was driven by AI and semiconductor profit-taking rather than a new broad macro shock: The Business Times reported rotation out of this year's best-performing technology shares and an Asian technology sub-gauge ending an eight-day winning run, while Reuters described a global selloff in technology and semiconductor shares as investors took profits on a long rally.

text: Oil provided cross-asset relief but did not stop the Asia equity unwind: Trading Economics put Brent at 76.92 USD/Bbl on June 23, down 1.26% on the day and 20.43% over the month, while Reuters reported oil below $80 as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz increased and physical market prices neared pre-conflict levels.

dominant_channels: oil settlement decline on resuming Hormuz flows
technology and semiconductor equity rout into the close
value.source_basis: official Treasury closing par-yield table plus dated post-close Reuters, NBC News, and Investopedia market reports

market_session: global_macro_session_with_us_regular_hours_close
text: June 23, 2026 was a normal Tuesday macro session. The verified source set shows a front-end-led Treasury rally, a dollar at a 13-month high, lower crude settlements as Strait of Hormuz flows resumed under a U.S.-Iran sanctions waiver, and a technology- and semiconductor-led equity selloff that deepened into the cash close.

market_session: treasury_par_yield_close_approx_1530_new_york_vs_2026-06-22

calculation.method: Treasury 06/23/2026 row minus 06/22/2026 row; 2s10s equals 10Y minus 2Y in basis points.

text: The official U.S. Treasury par-yield curve closed June 23, 2026 at 4.16% for 2Y, 4.50% for 10Y, and 4.94% for 30Y. Versus June 22, 2Y fell 8 bp while 10Y and 30Y each fell 1 bp, widening 2s10s from +27 bp to +34 bp.

drivers: hawkish Fed repricing under new Chair Kevin Warsh

market_session: new_york_fx_session_reuters_report
text: Reuters reported the U.S. Dollar Index rose 0.38% to 101.39 on June 23 after hitting 101.42, its highest since May 2025, with the euro down 0.41% at $1.138. CME FedWatch put July hike odds at 36.3% (up from 8.5% a week earlier) and September at 69.1% (up from 29.1%), as hawkish Fed repricing and a megacap selloff supported the dollar.

value.session_note: both benchmarks hit near-four-month lows during the session
drivers: resuming crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz
U.S. 60-day sanctions waiver after initial U.S.-Iran peace talks
Oman-Iran talks on Strait of Hormuz navigation administration

market_session: june_23_energy_futures_settlement
text: Reuters reported Brent futures closed down 82 cents, or 1.1%, at $77.08 per barrel on June 23, while U.S. WTI futures settled 65 cents, or 0.9%, lower at $73.21 per barrel; both benchmarks hit near-four-month lows as investors watched Hormuz flows after U.S.-Iran peace-talk progress and a 60-day sanctions waiver.

market_session: us_regular_session_close_reuters_eod_wrap
text: At the June 23 cash close, Reuters reported the Nasdaq Composite fell 579.56 points (-2.21%) to 25,587.04, the S&P 500 lost 107.32 points (-1.44%) to 7,365.47, and the Dow fell 47.22 points (-0.09%) to 51,665.49. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index tumbled 7.9% and the S&P 500 IT sector slipped 3.7%; Nvidia fell 4.1%, Micron and SanDisk each fell around 13%, and Intel, Marvell and AMD lost between 5.8% and 9.4%. The VIX rose 2.23 points to 19.52. NBC News and Investopedia corroborate the same closing moves.

subject.label: defensive rotation: stronger dollar, cheaper oil, front-end rates rally, tech-led equity rout

value.rates_signal: front-end-led bull steepening, 2s10s wider at +34 bp
value.fx_signal: dollar at a 13-month high on hawkish Fed repricing and safe-haven demand
value.oil_signal: near-four-month-low settlements as Hormuz flows resume, easing the inflation impulse
value.equity_signal: technology and semiconductor concentration carried a close-deepening risk-off move, with rotation into consumer staples

market_session: cross_asset_synthesis_from_june_23_source_set
text: June 23 was a defensive cross-asset rotation rather than a clean recession trade: crude settled at near-four-month lows as supply-route risk eased, the dollar hit a 13-month high on hawkish Fed repricing and safe-haven demand, Treasuries bull-steepened from the front end, and a technology- and semiconductor-led equity selloff deepened into the close while defensives such as consumer staples rose.

market_session: regular US cash-equity session, second session after Juneteenth closure
text: The June 23, 2026 report date was a regular Tuesday U.S. cash-equity session, the second session after the Juneteenth and weekend break, so sentiment values can be read against a live U.S. closing tape.

value.context: Big Tech and AI pullbacks; Micron Technology -13.2%; rate hike concerns weighing on growth

market_session: US cash equity close
text: AP reported a broadly lower June 23 U.S. close: the S&P 500 fell 107.33 points or 1.4% to 7,365.46 and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 579.56 points or 2.2% to 25,587.04 as Big Tech and AI-related names sold off. The Dow declined 45.87 points or 0.1% to 51,666.84 and the Russell 2000 fell 28.92 points or 1.0% to 2,975.48.

market_session: post-close sentiment reading
text: The Fear & Greed Index dropped to 28 (fear) on the June 23 post-close, down from approximately 33-35 at the June 22 close, after a 1.4% S&P 500 selloff. CNN's own page noted a methodology change to its Stock Price Breadth indicator.

market_session: Cboe published volatility-index data for the regular US session

calculation.method: VIX one-day percent change = (19.49 - 17.28) / 17.28 * 100 = 12.79%; curve compares Cboe close fields for VIX9D, VIX, VIX3M, and VIX6M on 2026-06-23

text: The Cboe VIX product page (data as of 8:15 PM ET June 23) and Yahoo Finance historical data showed VIX closing at 19.49 on June 23, up 2.21 points or 12.79% from 17.28 on June 22. The same-date curve was upward sloping with a flat front: VIX9D at 19.47, VIX at 19.49, VIX3M at 21.06, and VIX6M at 22.97. The VIX9D intraday range of 18.06 to 21.54 reflected early session panic that partially eased into the close.

value.data_freshness_note: Cboe daily statistics page accessed after June 23 close without a visible session date stamp; values matched the prior session's published readings at access time

market_session: Cboe daily market statistics page accessed after June 23 close; latest published session on the page
text: Cboe's daily market-statistics page, accessed after the June 23 close, showed total put/call at 0.92, index put/call at 1.08, ETP put/call at 1.15, equity put/call at 0.60, VIX put/call at 0.23, and SPX + SPXW put/call at 1.22. The page did not display a session date stamp, and the values matched the prior session's published readings at access time.

market_session: latest AAII weekly survey available for the June 23 report date

calculation.method: Bull-bear spread = bullish 36.6% minus bearish 39.4% = -2.8 percentage points

text: The latest AAII sentiment survey (week ending 6/17/2026, published June 18) showed bullish sentiment at 36.6%, neutral at 24.1%, and bearish at 39.4%, leaving a bull-bear spread of -2.8 percentage points. This is the same reading available for the June 22 report and does not yet reflect the June 23 selloff. The next AAII release is expected June 25.

value.data_freshness_note: No new weekly fund-flow releases between June 22 and June 23 report dates; both readings predate the June 23 selloff

market_session: latest fund-flow releases available for the June 23 report date
text: No new weekly fund-flow data was released between the June 22 and June 23 report dates. ICI reported $31.52 billion of long-term fund and ETF inflows for the week ended June 10, including $48.33 billion of ETF net issuance and $16.64 billion into equity funds. ETF.com's June 22 weekly article reported $119.83 billion of total ETF inflows led by $83.62 billion into U.S. equity ETFs. These readings provide positioning context heading into the June 23 selloff, not a real-time flow gauge.

value.volatility: VIX +12.79% to 19.49; upward-sloping curve with flat front
value.options: put/call page showed hedging readings but data freshness uncertain

value.risk_read: clear risk-off, question is one-day washout vs. start of correction

market_session: regular US cash-equity session synthesis from latest available sentiment indicators
text: The best synthesis for June 23 is 'deeper fear, rotation bid tested': the June 22 fragile-but-not-capitulation setup broke on June 23 as all four major indexes fell, VIX spiked nearly 13% to 19.49, and Fear & Greed dropped to 28. The Dow's narrow 0.1% decline offered a residual rotation signal, but the technology and AI selloff was severe enough to drag the entire tape lower. The absence of fresh AAII survey and flow data leaves open whether this is a one-day washout or the start of a deeper correction.
